data.title: Blogging veteran Sherry Bennett marks 29 years online, unveiling secrets to her 7-figure blog empire.
data.description: In Los Angeles, as of October 23, 2025, Sherry Bennett has been blogging for an impressive 29 years, a period predating the rise of social media influencers and content creators. Bennett's enduring presence in the online realm establishes her as a trailblazer.

data.title: Inventor from Boonville, Mo. creates new WINE GUARD cover for wine glasses to protect from insects & debris outdoors.
data.description: An inventor from Boonville, Missouri, shared their inspiration behind creating the WINE GUARD, a functional and decorative cover for wine glasses to shield them from insects and outdoor debris. This innovative solution was developed to address the need for protection when enjoying drinks outside. The WINE GUARD aims to offer a practical alternative to traditional methods, enhancing outdoor dining experiences. This invention introduces a unique approach with hopes to revolutionize the way individuals enjoy drinks outdoors.

data.title: Trump grants pardon to Binance founder CZ following Biden's crackdown on crypto, says White House.
data.description: The White House has confirmed that U.S. President Donald Trump has issued a pardon to Changpeng "CZ" Zhao, the founder of Binance. No specific reasons were provided for the pardon.

data.title: Energy Vault Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: NRGV) to hold Investor & Analyst Day on Oct 29, 2025, at 1:00 p.m. ET virtually.
data.description: Energy Vault Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: NRGV), a prominent player in sustainable grid-scale energy storage and infrastructure solutions, disclosed plans for a virtual Investor & Analyst Day scheduled on Wednesday, October 29th, 2025, at 1:00 p.m. ET. The event aims to provide crucial insights through presentations by key executives like Robert Piconi, Chairman. This announcement signifies a strategic move by the Company to engage with stakeholders and showcase its progress in the industry.

data.title: Kuwait and UN collaborate on 'Football for Humanity' initiative to aid Palestinian children, with support from UN agencies.
data.description: In Kuwait City on October 23, 2025, an announcement was made regarding the launch of the first edition of Football for Humanity, a humanitarian diplomacy initiative supported by the UN. This initiative is being undertaken in collaboration with key organizations including UN Kuwait, UNICEF, WHO, and UNESCO. The press conference, held with the patronage of Kuwait's Foreign Ministry, marked the beginning of this impactful partnership.

data.title: Peter Schiff acknowledges underestimation of public demand for Bitcoin, still sees it as 'gigantic pump and dump' destined to zero. OGs sold narrative to create demand.
data.description: Bitcoin critic Peter Schiff has admitted that he underestimated the public's interest in purchasing the cryptocurrency, yet he maintains his bearish view, calling it a "gigantic pump and dump" headed for zero. In an interview clip, Schiff recognized his mistake in underestimating people's eagerness to buy Bitcoin and the effective marketing strategies of its promoters. He attributed the surge in demand to early Bitcoin adopters, whom he referred to as "OGs," for successfully crafting a compelling narrative that convinced individuals to invest. Schiff alleged that these early holders capitalized on hype cycles to sell off their assets to retail investors. Despite acknowledging public demand for Bitcoin, Schiff remains firm in his belief that it will eventually become worthless. He shared these views in an interview available on a financial news website.

data.title: CancerCare Manitoba partners with Gray Oncology Solutions, implementing GrayOS to improve access to systemic therapy and efficiency in cancer care.
data.description: In October 23, 2025, Gray Oncology Solutions launched GrayOS software at CancerCare Manitoba (CCMB) to improve access to systemic therapy and increase efficiency in cancer care. CCMB has collaborated with Gray to implement this innovative technology, marking a significant advancement in cancer care delivery.

data.title: Freeport-McMoRan Q3 FY25: Revenue of $6.97B, EPS 50c, net income $674M, 912M lbs copper production, 287K oz gold.
data.description: Shares of Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (NYSE: FCX) are seeing an increase in premarket trading following the release of their third-quarter FY25 results, which exceeded expectations. The company reported revenue of $6.97 billion, surpassing the estimated $6.71 billion. Their adjusted EPS of 50 cents also outperformed the consensus of 41 cents. Net income attributable to common stock reached $674 million, translating to 46 cents per share during the quarter. In terms of production and sales volumes, Freeport-McMoRan reported 912 million pounds of copper production and 977 million pounds in sales. Gold production stood at 287,000 ounces with 336,000 ounces in sales. Additionally, molybdenum production totaled 22 million pounds, with 19 million pounds in sales. Financial analyst Jim Cramer also commented on the company's performance, expressing that it appears undervalued. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: ATOM may reach $4.15 in 4 weeks, showing a 30% potential upside. Technical indicators hint at oversold conditions.
data.description: The forecast for the price of ATOM indicates a possible increase of 30% to reach $4.15 in the next month. Despite signals of bearish momentum, technical indicators point to oversold conditions.

data.title: Dover Corp (DOV) beats Q3 earnings expectations with $2.078B revenue, 5% YoY increase, raises full-year guidance.
data.description: Dover Corporation (NYSE: DOV), a diversified global manufacturer, released its Q3 2025 financial results recently. The company's revenue stood at $2.078 billion, indicating a year-over-year increase of 5% or a 1% organic rise. Despite falling short of the analyst consensus estimate of $2.111 billion, Dover Corporation exceeded adjusted earnings expectations and raised its full-year guidance. The GAAP diluted EPS from continuing operations was reported at $2.20, marking a 3% decrease, while the adjusted diluted EPS from continuing operations came in at $2.62, surpassing the analyst estimate of $2.51. The company's profitability saw an uptick, with adjusted earnings reaching $361 million, a 15% increase, resulting in a record consolidated segment margin, driven by positive mix and rigorous cost containment. Management also expressed optimism due to strong order trends, providing clarity for the upcoming year. For further details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: OMODA & JAECOO revamp automotive ecosystem with flagship products, showcasing "One Store, Two Lifestyles" at 2025 summit in Malaysia and China.
data.description: In the 2025 OMODA & JAECOO International User Summit held in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia and WUHU, China on October 23, the brand showcased its "One Store, Two Lifestyles" concept in an Eco-exhibition. The innovative space featured smart technology integration and contemporary design, emphasizing sustainability and user experience. The event attracted a significant audience, with attendees from various industries and backgrounds engaging with the interactive displays and demonstrations. Overall, the summit was a success in promoting the brand's vision and commitment to innovation.

data.title: Premera Blue Cross responds to data security incident involving Conduent, a third-party service provider.
data.description: Conduent Business Services, LLC, located in Mountlake Terrace, Washington, recently announced on October 23, 2025, that they specialize in various third-party services including printing/mailroom services, document processing services, payment integrity services, government benefit services, and other back-office operations.

data.title: Community Heritage Financial, Inc. reports $1.9M net income in Q3 2025, $0.66 per share.
data.description: In Middletown, Maryland, on October 23, 2025, Community Heritage Financial, Inc. (CHF) disclosed its financial results. The parent company of Middletown Valley Bank (MVB) recorded a net income of $1.9 million for the third quarter, translating to earnings of $0.66 per diluted share.

data.title: Healthcare leads AI adoption at 2.2x pace of other industries, spending $1.4B in 2025, with 22% adopting domain-specific solutions like medical documentation and revenue cycle management.Providers account for $1B of spending, with ambient clinical documentation and coding automation as top categories. Patient engagement and prior authorization show significant growth. Health systems lead adoption at 27%, followed by outpatient providers (18%) and payers (14%). Healthcare has 8 AI unicorns, surpassing other AI vertical categories.
data.description: A report by Menlo Ventures reveals that AI spending in healthcare reached $1.4 billion in 2025, nearly triple last year's total, with healthcare organizations adopting AI 2.2x faster than the broader economy. 22% of healthcare organizations have deployed domain-specific AI solutions, a 7x increase from 2024, led by health systems (27%), outpatient providers (18%), and payers (14%). The industry boasts eight AI unicorns in medical documentation, revenue cycle management, and payer operations. $1 billion of the AI spend is in production, with top categories being ambient clinical documentation ($600M) and coding and billing automation ($450M). Patient engagement and prior authorization show significant growth rates. Health systems have shorter buying cycles for AI tools compared to payers. This data signals an inflection point in AI adoption within the healthcare industry.
